overview.tagline: Bergen County's smallest and most exclusive residential enclave
overview.description: Rockleigh is the smallest borough in Bergen County by population, a quiet, exclusive residential community tucked along the New York State border in the northeastern corner of the county. With only a few hundred residents spread across large, wooded estates, Rockleigh offers a level of privacy, open space, and natural beauty that is virtually unmatched in Bergen County. The borough has no commercial district and no school system of its own — it is purely residential. Rockleigh is also home to the former Rockleigh Country Club and several corporate campuses along the Palisades Interstate Parkway corridor.
overview.schoolDistrict: Northern Valley Regional High School District (sends to Northvale/Norwood K-8 schools)
highlights: Smallest municipality by population in Bergen County
Large, wooded estate properties with exceptional privacy
Adjacent to Palisades Interstate Park for hiking and nature
No commercial development — purely residential character
Minutes from the Alpine and Tappan areas along the NY border

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Rockleigh features large estate-sized lots, many over an acre, with high-end custom homes set in wooded, private settings.
realEstate.recentTrends: Rockleigh's real estate market is extremely small with very few transactions per year. When properties become available, they attract buyers seeking privacy, large lots, and exclusivity. The unique nature of Rockleigh means traditional market trends are less applicable — each sale is essentially a one-of-a-kind transaction.
neighborhoodNotes: Rockleigh Road — Main residential road with estate homes on large lots
Paris Avenue area — Wooded properties along quiet lanes
Palisades Interstate Parkway vicinity — Properties near the parkway with wooded buffers
Northern section — Closest to the New York State border

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Trash collection is provided by a private hauler contracted by the Borough.
details: Pickup occurs weekly on a designated day
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by arrangement
Contact Borough Hall for schedule information

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling collected on a regular schedule.
details: Recyclables collected weekly or bi-weekly depending on schedule
Paper, cardboard, glass, cans, and plastics accepted
Yard waste may be composted on-site due to large lot sizes
Contact Borough Hall for specific recycling schedule

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Some properties are served by municipal water while others rely on private wells. Septic systems are common.
details: Municipal water service available in some areas
Many properties use private wells and septic systems
Septic systems must comply with Bergen County Health Department regulations
Well water testing required per state regulations
Contact Borough Hall for service area information

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for new construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board
Minimum lot sizes enforced to maintain the borough's residential character
Environmental permits may be required for properties near wetlands
Tree removal permits required in certain circumstances

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: As a small residential borough with no commercial district, parking regulations are minimal.
details: Residential properties have private driveways and garages
No commercial parking facilities in the borough
Snow emergency parking regulations may apply on borough roads
No metered parking anywhere in Rockleigh

additionalServices.title: Nearby Libraries
additionalServices.description: Rockleigh residents use neighboring town libraries through BCCLS reciprocal borrowing.
details: Northvale Public Library — closest library facility
Norwood Public Library — also nearby
Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS) card honored at all member libraries
Online digital resources available through BCCLS

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Rockleigh enforces noise regulations to preserve the borough's quiet, residential character.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 6:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM only
No construction noise on Sundays or federal holidays
Amplified music and loud gatherings restricted
The borough's quiet, rural character is emphasized in enforcement

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their estates in good condition.
keyRules: Properties must be maintained in a neat and orderly condition
Driveways and private roads must be maintained by property owners
Dead or hazardous trees must be removed promptly
Structures must be maintained in good repair
Fences and walls must comply with borough regulations

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dog licensing and leash laws apply throughout the borough.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed annually with rabies vaccination proof
Dogs must be under owner control at all times
Pet waste must be managed on private property
Wildlife encounters common — pets should be supervised outdoors
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Minimal parking regulations due to the residential nature of the borough.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough roads
Snow emergency bans may be declared
Commercial vehicles prohibited from parking on residential streets
Vehicles must not obstruct borough roads or rights-of-way

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental activity is minimal in this predominantly owner-occupied community.
keyRules: Rental properties must comply with all borough zoning regulations
Certificate of Occupancy required for all dwelling units
Short-term rentals may be restricted under zoning
Property owner remains responsible for maintenance and code compliance

other.title: Environmental and Land Use Regulations
other.summary: Rockleigh has strict land use regulations to preserve its wooded, estate character.
keyRules: Large minimum lot sizes enforced (typically 1-2 acres or more)
Tree preservation ordinances protect significant specimens
Wetlands and stream buffers must be maintained
Impervious surface coverage limits enforced
New development must preserve the natural landscape to the extent possible

schools.publicDistrict: Northern Valley Regional High School District (K-8 via sending arrangement)

publicSchools.name: Northvale Public School
publicSchools.notes: Rockleigh students attend Northvale Public School for elementary and middle school through a sending arrangement

publicSchools.name: Northern Valley Regional High School at Demarest
publicSchools.notes: Regional high school serving Rockleigh along with Closter, Demarest, Haworth, and Norwood

privateSchools.name: Elisabeth Morrow School (Englewood)
privateSchools.notes: Prestigious independent school nearby in Englewood

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 20 minutes
Columbia University (NYC) — accessible via Palisades Interstate Parkway

whyMoveHere: Unparalleled privacy on large, wooded estate lots
Smallest borough in Bergen County with exclusive, close-knit community
Adjacent to Palisades Interstate Park for hiking and nature
Access to excellent Northern Valley Regional High School
Low-density zoning ensures preservation of the natural landscape

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: Drive to Palisades Interstate Parkway / Route 9W
commuteToNYC.duration: 40-60 minutes to Midtown Manhattan by car
commuteToNYC.notes: Rockleigh is car-dependent for commuting. The Palisades Interstate Parkway provides scenic highway access to the George Washington Bridge. Some residents drive to nearby NJ Transit bus stops or the Alpine Boat Basin area. There is no train station in or near the borough.

costOfLiving.summary: Rockleigh is an affluent community with very high property values due to its large lots and exclusive character. The cost of living is well above state and national averages.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 75% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Maximum privacy with large, wooded estate lots
Quiet, exclusive community with very low density
Beautiful natural surroundings and proximity to the Palisades
Low property tax rate (though high assessments)
Access to well-regarded Northern Valley schools
cons: Completely car-dependent with no public transit within the borough
No shops, restaurants, or commercial services in town
Very limited housing inventory — rarely anything available
No local schools — students must be transported to other towns
Septic and well systems require private maintenance on many properties

restaurants.name: The Restaurant at The Rockleigh
restaurants.description: Upscale dining at the Rockleigh estate venue, offering refined cuisine in elegant surroundings for special occasions and events.

restaurants.name: Restaurants in Alpine/Closter (nearby)
restaurants.description: The nearby communities of Alpine and Closter offer various dining options within a short drive of Rockleigh.

restaurants.name: Old Tappan dining (nearby)
restaurants.description: Neighboring Old Tappan offers several restaurants and cafes easily accessible from Rockleigh.

parks.name: Palisades Interstate Park
parks.description: Spectacular park along the Hudson River Palisades offering hiking trails, scenic overlooks, picnic areas, and historic sites. One of the premier outdoor destinations in the region.

parks.name: Tallman Mountain State Park (NY)
parks.description: New York State park just across the border from Rockleigh with hiking trails, cross-country skiing, and Hudson River views.

parks.name: Rockleigh Borough Green Spaces
parks.description: The borough itself features extensive wooded areas and natural landscapes that contribute to its pastoral character.

events.name: Events at The Rockleigh
events.description: The Rockleigh estate venue hosts weddings, galas, corporate events, and seasonal celebrations in a stunning garden setting.

events.name: Borough Community Gathering
events.description: Small annual community gathering for Rockleigh residents, reflecting the borough's intimate scale.

attractions.name: The Rockleigh
attractions.description: Stunning event venue and estate featuring manicured gardens, elegant ballrooms, and a historic property that hosts weddings and special events.

attractions.name: Palisades Interstate Park — State Line Lookout
attractions.description: Scenic overlook on the Palisades with panoramic views of the Hudson River and New York. Located along the Palisades Interstate Parkway near Rockleigh.

attractions.name: Lamont-Doherty Earth Observatory (nearby)
attractions.description: Columbia University's earth science research facility in Palisades, NY, just across the border. Occasionally hosts public lectures and events.

shoppingAreas: Closter Plaza (Closter) — Nearest shopping center with grocery and retail
Northvale — Small shops and services nearby
Old Tappan — Local shops and restaurants
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Major regional mall, 20 minutes south

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the 3rd Wednesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 26 Rockleigh Road, Rockleigh, NJ 07647

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
